text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. They involve team members carefully examining each other's code before it is merged into the main project. One major benefit is catching bugs early. When fresh eyes look at code, they often spot errors or potential problems that the original writer missed, which saves time and money later. Code reviews also promote knowledge sharing. Experienced developers can suggest better approaches, while junior team members learn new techniques and best practices. Ultimately, this process improves overall team quality. Everyone becomes more familiar with the codebase, standards are maintained, and the whole team grows stronger together.
